Package cc.quaternion.potshaper

Class PotPresentation

java.lang.Object
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotPresentation
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable
public class PotPresentation extends Object implements AutoCloseable
The central class for creating, opening, and manipulating PowerPoint presentations.

This class serves as the main entry point for the PotShaper library, providing a high-level, fluent API for slide management, content addition, and presentation persistence. It wraps the underlying Apache POI XMLSlideShow and manages the lifecycle of slides and elements. Each presentation maintains internal registries for slides and elements using UUIDs for identification, supports custom page sizes, and offers utilities for rendering and metadata management.

Usage Example


 // Create a new presentation with a default widescreen slide
 PotPresentation ppt = PotPresentation.create();

 // Open an existing presentation from a file
 PotPresentation ppt = PotPresentation.open("test.pptx");

 // Add a text box to the first slide
 PotSlide slide = ppt.getSlide(0);
 slide.addTextBox("Hello").at(100, 100);

 // Save the presentation and release resources
 ppt.save("output.pptx");
 ppt.close();
Since:
1.0
Author:
Quaternion8192

  • Method Details

    • open

      public static PotPresentation open(String path)
      Opens an existing presentation from the specified file path.

      This method loads the presentation file from disk and creates a new PotPresentation instance. The file must exist and be a valid PowerPoint (.pptx) file.

      Parameters:
      path - the file system path to the presentation file
      Returns:
      a new PotPresentation instance representing the opened file
      Throws:
      PotException - if the file does not exist, cannot be read, or is not a valid presentation
      See Also:
      • open(File)
      • open(InputStream)

    • open

      public static PotPresentation open(File file)
      Opens an existing presentation from the specified file.

      This method loads the presentation file from disk and creates a new PotPresentation instance. The file must exist and be a valid PowerPoint (.pptx) file.

      Parameters:
      file - the presentation file to open
      Returns:
      a new PotPresentation instance representing the opened file
      Throws:
      PotException - if the file does not exist, cannot be read, or is not a valid presentation
      See Also:
      • open(String)
      • open(InputStream)

    • open

      public static PotPresentation open(InputStream inputStream)
      Opens an existing presentation from an input stream.

      This method reads the presentation data from the provided stream and creates a new PotPresentation instance. The stream must contain valid PowerPoint (.pptx) data. The caller is responsible for managing the stream's lifecycle; it will be read from but not closed by this method.

      Parameters:
      inputStream - the input stream containing presentation data
      Returns:
      a new PotPresentation instance representing the opened data
      Throws:
      PotException - if the stream cannot be read or does not contain valid presentation data
      See Also:
      • open(String)
      • open(File)

    • create

      public static PotPresentation create()
      Creates a new blank presentation with a default widescreen (16:9) page size.

      The new presentation will contain a single empty slide. The page size is set to PotPageSize.WIDESCREEN_16_9.

      Returns:
      a new PotPresentation instance with one empty slide
      Throws:
      PotException - if the underlying Apache POI library fails to initialize
      See Also:
      • create(PotPageSize)
      • create(PotPageSize.CustomPageSize)

    • create

      public static PotPresentation create(PotPageSize pageSize)
      Creates a new blank presentation with the specified standard page size.

      The new presentation will contain a single empty slide. If pageSize is null or PotPageSize.CUSTOM, the default Apache POI page size is used.

      Parameters:
      pageSize - the standard page size to apply; may be null
      Returns:
      a new PotPresentation instance with one empty slide
      Throws:
      PotException - if the underlying Apache POI library fails to initialize
      See Also:
      • create()
      • create(PotPageSize.CustomPageSize)

    • create

      public static PotPresentation create(PotPageSize.CustomPageSize customSize)
      Creates a new blank presentation with a custom page size.

      The new presentation will contain a single empty slide. The page dimensions are taken from the provided CustomPageSize object.

      Parameters:
      customSize - the custom page dimensions; may be null
      Returns:
      a new PotPresentation instance with one empty slide
      Throws:
      PotException - if the underlying Apache POI library fails to initialize
      See Also:
      • create()
      • create(PotPageSize)

    • save

      public void save()
      Saves the presentation to its original source file.

      This method writes the current state of the presentation back to the file from which it was opened. If the presentation was created in memory (i.e., has no source file), a PotException is thrown.

      Throws:
      PotException - if the presentation has no source file, is closed, or an I/O error occurs
      See Also:
      • save(String)
      • save(File)
      • save(OutputStream)

    • save

      public void save(String path)
      Saves the presentation to the specified file path.

      This method writes the current state of the presentation to a new file. If a file already exists at the given path, it will be overwritten.

      Parameters:
      path - the file system path where the presentation should be saved
      Throws:
      PotException - if the presentation is closed or an I/O error occurs
      See Also:
      • save()
      • save(File)
      • save(OutputStream)

    • saveAs

      public void saveAs(String path)
      Saves the presentation to a new file (alias for save(String)).

      This method provides a fluent API alternative to save(String). It behaves identically.

      Parameters:
      path - the file system path where the presentation should be saved
      Throws:
      PotException - if the presentation is closed or an I/O error occurs
      See Also:
      • save(String)

    • saveAs

      public void saveAs(File file)
      Saves the presentation to a new file (alias for save(File)).

      This method provides a fluent API alternative to save(File). It behaves identically.

      Parameters:
      file - the file where the presentation should be saved
      Throws:
      PotException - if the presentation is closed or an I/O error occurs
      See Also:
      • save(File)

    • save

      public void save(File file)
      Saves the presentation to the specified file.

      This method writes the current state of the presentation to the given file. If the file already exists, it will be overwritten.

      Parameters:
      file - the file where the presentation should be saved
      Throws:
      PotException - if the presentation is closed or an I/O error occurs
      See Also:
      • save()
      • save(String)
      • save(OutputStream)

    • save

      public void save(OutputStream outputStream)
      Saves the presentation to the specified output stream.

      This method writes the current state of the presentation to the provided output stream. The caller is responsible for closing the stream after this method returns.

      Parameters:
      outputStream - the output stream to write the presentation data to
      Throws:
      PotException - if the presentation is closed or an I/O error occurs
      See Also:
      • save()
      • save(String)
      • save(File)

    • save

      public void save(String path, SaveOptions options)
      Saves the presentation to the specified file path with additional options.

      This method writes the current state of the presentation to a new file, applying the provided SaveOptions such as backup creation, buffering, and web optimization.

      Parameters:
      path - the file system path where the presentation should be saved
      options - the configuration options for the save operation; may be null
      Throws:
      PotException - if the presentation is closed or an I/O error occurs
      See Also:
      • save(File, SaveOptions)
      • save(OutputStream, SaveOptions)

    • save

      public void save(File file, SaveOptions options)
      Saves the presentation to the specified file with additional options.

      This method writes the current state of the presentation to the given file, applying the provided SaveOptions such as backup creation, buffering, and web optimization.

      Parameters:
      file - the file where the presentation should be saved
      options - the configuration options for the save operation; may be null
      Throws:
      PotException - if the presentation is closed or an I/O error occurs
      See Also:
      • save(String, SaveOptions)
      • save(OutputStream, SaveOptions)

    • save

      public void save(OutputStream outputStream, SaveOptions options)
      Saves the presentation to the specified output stream with additional options.

      This method writes the current state of the presentation to the provided output stream, applying the given SaveOptions such as buffering and web optimization.

      Parameters:
      outputStream - the output stream to write the presentation data to
      options - the configuration options for the save operation; may be null
      Throws:
      PotException - if the presentation is closed or an I/O error occurs
      See Also:
      • save(String, SaveOptions)
      • save(File, SaveOptions)

    • close

      public void close()
      Closes the presentation and releases all underlying resources.

      This method closes the internal Apache POI XMLSlideShow and clears all internal registries. After calling this method, the presentation cannot be used further. If the presentation is already closed, this method does nothing.

      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Throws:
      PotException - if an I/O error occurs while closing the underlying resources

    • getMemoryStats

      public MemoryStats getMemoryStats()
      Calculates and returns memory usage statistics for this presentation.

      This method estimates the memory footprint based on the number of slides, shapes, and media elements. The estimation uses predefined constants and provides a rough guide for memory management.

      Returns:
      a MemoryStats object containing slide, shape, and media counts with an estimated memory usage in megabytes

    • setLargeFileWarning

      public void setLargeFileWarning(int thresholdMB, Consumer<MemoryStats> listener)
      Sets a warning listener that triggers when the presentation's estimated memory exceeds a threshold.

      This method immediately checks the current memory usage and invokes the provided listener if the estimated memory exceeds the specified threshold. The listener receives the current MemoryStats for further inspection.

      Parameters:
      thresholdMB - the memory threshold in megabytes; if the estimated memory exceeds this, the listener is invoked
      listener - a consumer that accepts MemoryStats when the threshold is exceeded; may be null

    • getPageWidth

      public double getPageWidth()
      Returns the current page width of the presentation in points.

      One point is 1/72 of an inch. This value corresponds to the width of the slides.

      Returns:
      the page width in points

    • getPageHeight

      public double getPageHeight()
      Returns the current page height of the presentation in points.

      One point is 1/72 of an inch. This value corresponds to the height of the slides.

      Returns:
      the page height in points

    • setPageSize

      public void setPageSize(PotPageSize pageSize)
      Sets the page size of the presentation using a standard page size enumeration.

      This method changes the dimensions of all slides in the presentation. If pageSize is null or PotPageSize.CUSTOM, no change is made.

      Parameters:
      pageSize - the standard page size to apply; may be null

    • setPageSize

      public void setPageSize(double width, double height)
      Sets a custom page size for the presentation.

      This method changes the dimensions of all slides in the presentation to the specified width and height in points.

      Parameters:
      width - the new page width in points
      height - the new page height in points

    • getSlideCount

      public int getSlideCount()
      Returns the total number of slides in the presentation.
      Returns:
      the slide count

    • getSlide

      public PotSlide getSlide(int index)
      Retrieves the slide at the specified index.

      Slides are zero-indexed. The returned PotSlide object provides methods for adding and manipulating content on that slide.

      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based index of the slide to retrieve
      Returns:
      the PotSlide at the given index
      Throws:
      PotException - if the index is out of bounds (less than 0 or greater than or equal to the slide count)

    • getSlides

      public List<PotSlide> getSlides()
      Returns an unmodifiable list of all slides in the presentation.

      The list is in the same order as the slides appear in the presentation.

      Returns:
      a list of all PotSlide objects

    • addSlide

      public PotSlide addSlide()
      Appends a new blank slide to the end of the presentation.

      The new slide uses the default layout from the presentation's master. It is assigned a unique UUID and registered internally.

      Returns:
      the newly created PotSlide

    • insertSlide

      public PotSlide insertSlide(int index)
      Inserts a new blank slide at the specified index.

      If the index is equal to or greater than the current slide count, the slide is appended to the end. The slide is assigned a unique UUID and registered internally.

      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based index at which to insert the new slide
      Returns:
      the newly created PotSlide

    • cloneSlide

      public PotSlide cloneSlide(int sourceIndex)
      Creates a duplicate of an existing slide and appends it to the presentation.

      This method copies the content and layout of the source slide. The new slide is assigned a unique UUID and registered internally.

      Parameters:
      sourceIndex - the zero-based index of the slide to clone
      Returns:
      the newly created PotSlide that is a copy of the source
      Throws:
      PotException - if the source index is out of bounds

    • removeSlide

      public void removeSlide(int index)
      Removes the slide at the specified index from the presentation.

      The slide is unregistered from the internal registry, and all subsequent slides shift up by one position.

      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based index of the slide to remove
      Throws:
      PotException - if the index is out of bounds

    • moveSlide

      public void moveSlide(int fromIndex, int toIndex)
      Moves a slide from one position to another within the presentation.

      This method reorders slides. All indices must be valid. If fromIndex equals toIndex, no action is taken.

      Parameters:
      fromIndex - the current zero-based index of the slide to move
      toIndex - the new zero-based index for the slide
      Throws:
      PotException - if either index is out of bounds

    • findElement

      public PotElement findElement(String uuid)
      Finds a presentation element by its unique identifier.

      This method searches the internal registry for an element with the given UUID. If no matching element is found, null is returned.

      Parameters:
      uuid - the UUID string of the element to find
      Returns:
      the PotElement with the specified UUID, or null

    • findSlide

      public PotSlide findSlide(String uuid)
      Finds a slide by its unique identifier.

      This method searches the internal registry for a slide with the given UUID. If no matching slide is found, null is returned.

      Parameters:
      uuid - the UUID string of the slide to find
      Returns:
      the PotSlide with the specified UUID, or null

    • getMasters

      public List<PotMaster> getMasters()
      Returns a list of all slide masters defined in the presentation.

      Slide masters define the overall design and layout templates for slides. Each master is wrapped in a PotMaster object.

      Returns:
      a list of PotMaster objects

    • getLayouts

      public List<PotLayout> getLayouts()
      Returns a list of all slide layouts available across all masters.

      Slide layouts are templates within a master that define placeholder positions and formatting. Each layout is wrapped in a PotLayout object.

      Returns:
      a list of PotLayout objects

    • exportJsonIndex

      public String exportJsonIndex()
      Exports a JSON representation of the presentation's structure and metadata.

      The JSON includes slide indices, element UUIDs, and basic properties, suitable for indexing or external processing.

      Returns:
      a JSON string representing the presentation index

    • renderSlide

      public BufferedImage renderSlide(int index)
      Renders a slide as a raster image at its native size.

      This method draws the specified slide onto a BufferedImage with a scale factor of 1.0. The image background is filled with white.

      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based index of the slide to render
      Returns:
      a BufferedImage containing the rendered slide
      Throws:
      PotException - if the index is out of bounds

    • renderSlide

      public BufferedImage renderSlide(int index, double scale)
      Renders a slide as a raster image at a specified scale.

      This method draws the specified slide onto a BufferedImage, scaling the output by the given factor. The image background is filled with white, and high-quality rendering hints are applied.

      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based index of the slide to render
      scale - the scaling factor (e.g., 2.0 for double size, 0.5 for half size)
      Returns:
      a BufferedImage containing the rendered slide
      Throws:
      PotException - if the index is out of bounds

    • getRawSlideShow

      public org.apache.poi.xslf.usermodel.XMLSlideShow getRawSlideShow()
      Provides direct access to the underlying Apache POI XMLSlideShow object.

      This method is intended for advanced use cases requiring low-level POI operations. Modifications made directly to the returned object may affect the integrity of this PotPresentation wrapper.

      Returns:
      the internal XMLSlideShow instance

    • getSourceFile

      public File getSourceFile()
      Returns the source file from which this presentation was opened, if any.

      If the presentation was created from scratch or loaded from a stream, this method returns null.

      Returns:
      the source File, or null

    • xml

      public PotXml xml()
      Returns a helper object for low-level XML manipulation of the presentation.

      The returned PotXml provides methods to access and modify the underlying XML structures of the presentation, such as slide relationships and shape properties.

      Returns:
      a PotXml instance bound to this presentation

    • setTitle

      public void setTitle(String title)
      Sets the title of the presentation.

      The title is stored in the document's core properties and may be displayed by presentation viewers.

      Parameters:
      title - the new title (may be null)

    • getTitle

      public String getTitle()
      Returns the title of the presentation.

      If no title has been set, this method returns null.

      Returns:
      the presentation title, or null

    • setAuthor

      public void setAuthor(String author)
      Sets the author of the presentation.

      The author is stored in the document's core properties and may be displayed by presentation viewers.

      Parameters:
      author - the new author (may be null)

    • getAuthor

      public String getAuthor()
      Returns the author of the presentation.

      If no author has been set, this method returns null.

      Returns:
      the presentation author, or null

    • setSubject

      public void setSubject(String subject)
      Sets the subject of the presentation.

      The subject is stored in the document's core properties and may be displayed by presentation viewers.

      Parameters:
      subject - the new subject (may be null)

    • getSubject

      public String getSubject()
      Returns the subject of the presentation.

      If no subject has been set, this method returns null.

      Returns:
      the presentation subject, or null

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
